evals.prompt: Generate a hero image for the blog post about AI meeting intelligence. Dark tech aesthetic, 16:9 aspect ratio. Something abstract with interconnected nodes.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that authenticates with Higgsfield, generates an image with proper params, downloads it, and saves to the correct project location.

assertions.text: Script loads .env with dotenv and maps HIGGSFIELDS_API_KEY to HF_API_KEY
Model is 'higgsfield-ai/soul/standard' or another valid model ID
Downloads the result image via urllib or requests
Saves to blog/images/ or images/blog/ with descriptive filename
Wraps generation in try/except for error handling

evals.prompt: Make me a square image for a social media post about our headless CMS migration services. Professional but not boring.
evals.expected_output: Generates a 1:1 image with appropriate prompt and saves it.

assertions.text: aspect_ratio is '1:1' for social media square
Prompt is detailed and specific (not just 'headless CMS image')
Auth mapping from HIGGSFIELDS_* to HF_* is present

evals.prompt: I have an existing screenshot of our dashboard at images/screenshots/command-center.png. Edit it to add a dramatic sunset sky in the background window reflection.
evals.expected_output: Uses the edit model (bytedance/seedream/v4/edit) with upload_file to modify the existing image.

assertions.text: Uses upload_file() to upload the source image first
Passes the uploaded URL as 'image_url' in arguments
Downloads and saves the edited result
